registry_note: availability values: IMPLEMENTED (existing config switch on the real production spine, verified against canonical_pipeline.md), NOT_IMPLEMENTED (would require new code -- listed honestly, not fabricated as working), PARTIAL (existing primitive exists but not wired as a standalone selectable method). No method silently falls back to another: any NOT_IMPLEMENTED method invoked by a future command must hard-fail, per split_guard-style rejection, not substitute a different method.

PROPOSED_FULL.name: Complete frozen production pipeline
PROPOSED_FULL.layers: L1=production admission, L2=scene_sparse+PPR (ppr_lambda=0.5 default), L3=legacy answerer+CerberusV verification
PROPOSED_FULL.failure_behavior: hard-fail on ingest/query exception, no silent fallback

L1_uniform_budget_matched.layers: L1=uniform sampling at same retention budget K
L1_uniform_budget_matched.availability: NOT_IMPLEMENTED -- retrieval_strategy has no 'uniform' option (only peak_only/top_k_action/peak_neighbors/hybrid), needs new code

L1_random_budget_matched_30seeds.layers: L1=random sampling at same K, 30 fixed seeds
L1_random_budget_matched_30seeds.availability: NOT_IMPLEMENTED -- needs new code + seed list registered in configs/

L1_action_only.layers: L1=admission driven by action_score alone (packet/motion/luma weights zeroed except one)
L1_action_only.availability: PARTIAL -- ActionScoreModule weights are config fields (packet_size_weight, motion_weight, luma_entropy_weight); setting the other two to 0 approximates this without new code, but is not currently a named preset

L1_packet_size_only.layers: L1=admission driven by packet_size signal only

L1_luma_difference_only.availability: NOT_IMPLEMENTED -- luma_diff_energy is not one of ActionScoreModule's 3 weighted inputs (those are packet_size, motion_magnitude, luma_entropy -- luma_diff_energy is a separate stored field, not currently a weight input)

L1_scene_change_only.availability: PARTIAL -- compute_valley_scene_boundaries exists; wiring it as a standalone admission rule needs new code

L1_farneback_optical_flow.layers: L1=Farneback dense optical flow replacing codec motion vectors
L1_farneback_optical_flow.availability: NOT_IMPLEMENTED -- charon_v uses codec-native MVs, not Farneback; would require full-decode + cv2.calcOpticalFlowFarneback integration

L1_no_luma_entropy.availability: IMPLEMENTED via config (packet_size_weight/motion_weight renormalized to sum 1.0)

L1_no_luma_difference.layers: L1=admission with luma_diff_energy excluded from any downstream selection heuristic
L1_no_luma_difference.availability: PARTIAL -- luma_diff_energy is not an ActionScoreModule weight input already (see L1_luma_difference_only note), so 'removing' it may be a no-op depending on where else it's consumed; needs code audit before claiming implemented

L1_no_motion_geometry.layers: L1=compute_motion_geometry outputs excluded from graph motion-neighbor edges
L1_no_motion_geometry.availability: PARTIAL -- would need graph_motion_top_k=0 (removes motion-neighbor edges at L2, not strictly an L1 change) or new code to zero geometry fields at L1

L1_pass1_only.layers: L1=zero-decode packet-curve pass only, no Pass-2 decode/feature extraction
L1_pass1_only.availability: NOT_IMPLEMENTED -- parse_video's two passes are not independently exposed as a selectable mode

L2_legacy_sparse_direct.layers: L2=ranking_mode=legacy (alpha/beta/gamma/delta weighted direct scoring, no PPR)

L2_clip_query_topk.layers: L2=direct CLIP cosine top-K over the L1 survivor pool, no graph/PPR
L2_clip_query_topk.category: fair-baseline (this is the real 'L2 OFF' cell, see F0xx factorial notes)
L2_clip_query_topk.availability: IMPLEMENTED via config: ranking_mode='legacy' with alpha=1.0,beta=gamma=delta=0.0 reduces L2Asphodel.retrieve to pure semantic-cosine ranking

L2_action_persistence_direct_rank.layers: L2=legacy ranking with alpha=0, beta+gamma covering motion/persistence, delta=0

L2_ppr_peak_legacy.availability: IMPLEMENTED (peak selection here is just top-1 PPR output, no new code)

L2_no_temporal_edges.availability: NOT_IMPLEMENTED -- _add_temporal_edges is not independently togglable via config today (would need a new graph_edge_mode variant or a boolean flag)

L2_no_cross_scene_edges.availability: PARTIAL -- add_cross_scene_edges only runs in scene_retrieval's DESCEND branch; need to confirm a config value fully suppresses the call vs. just changes its mode

L3_full_answerer_verifier.layers: L3=legacy answerer + CerberusV verification + abstention (== PROPOSED_FULL default)

L3_no_verifier.layers: L3=answerer output returned without CerberusV verification/abstention gate
L3_no_verifier.category: ablation (this is the real 'L3 OFF' cell)
L3_no_verifier.availability: NOT_IMPLEMENTED -- no existing config flag bypasses wrapper_cerberus_gate while keeping the same answerer call; disable_nli only disables the NLI sub-component of CerberusV, not the whole gate. Needs new code (a query.py code path variant).

L3_no_abstention.layers: L3=verifier runs, but abstention message suppressed (always return best-effort claims)

L3_raw_answerer.layers: L3=ARIA answerer called directly on retrieved-frame captions with no claim contract / verification at all

L3_captions_without_timestamps.layers: L3=strip timestamp text from caption context before answering
L3_captions_without_timestamps.availability: PARTIAL -- caption context assembly is in L1ElysiumCache.as_context_text; needs a formatting-only code change

L3_shuffled_caption_negative_control.layers: L3=randomly permute caption order before answering

L3_query_blind_negative_control.layers: L3=answerer given captions but not the question text

L3_gold_span_oracle.layers: L3=captioning restricted to frames inside the gold temporal span
L3_gold_span_oracle.category: ORACLE -- MUST BE EXCLUDED FROM FAIR SUPERIORITY TABLES
L3_gold_span_oracle.availability: NOT_IMPLEMENTED, and requires gold spans which are not verified at official scale (see dataset_manifest.json)

schema_note: every method entry's eventual runnable config must additionally record: unique_method_id (the JSON key above), candidate_pool, budget_rule, required_models, required_cached_features, config_file path under configs/methods/<id>.json, expected_output_schema=schemas/per_question_result.json (see artifact_schema.json), and failure_behavior=hard_fail_no_fallback. Concrete configs/methods/*.json files are deferred until each NOT_IMPLEMENTED method's new code exists, to avoid registering a config for a code path that would silently no-op.
